title: ECP Cluster Emission v1
description: Canonical shape of what a v2 specialist auditor (one per cluster per device) emits to disk. Replaces the v1 cluster-{cluster}-{device}.md markdown file. JSON in; JSON out; no prose parsing. Reconciler reads these files via JSON Schema validation, not regex against finding-block prose. Authored 2026-04-27 brainstorm; pairs with finding-v1.json. See docs/brainstorms/2026-04-27-ecp-v2-architecture-brainstorm.md.

schema_version.description: Version of the cluster-emission schema. v1 is current. Bump on backward-incompatible changes to the emission shape.

engagement_id.description: The audit engagement this emission belongs to. Format: YYYY-MM-DD-<8 hex>. Reconciler verifies this matches the parent meta.json.id.

cluster.description: Which cluster this specialist audited. Must match the dispatch slug. Ethics emissions use cluster='ethics'.

device.description: Which device this specialist audited. Ethics specialists may use 'page' to indicate a single emission across both devices (consumed identically by both device assemblies).

specialist_model.description: Which model produced the findings. Useful for cost/quality telemetry and for parity-test comparisons across model versions.

version.description: Model version string (e.g., '4.6', '4.7'). Free-form to accommodate future versions; the synthesizer treats this as opaque.

context_window.description: Optional. The context window the specialist ran in. Accepts string form ('1M', '200K') or integer form (200000, 1000000). Useful when comparing context-window-conditioned behavior across runs.

temperature.description: Optional. Sampling temperature the specialist ran at. v2 default is low (0.0-0.2) for determinism.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the specialist began. Used for runtime telemetry and parity-test comparisons.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the specialist finished writing this file. completed_at - started_at gives the wall-clock runtime.

status.description: How the specialist run resolved. 'complete' = all findings emitted normally. 'partial' = ran but found a degraded condition (e.g., DOM slice missing critical sections). 'skipped' = specialist did not run; common for clusters with empty cluster-context post-pruning.

skip_reason.description: Required when status='skipped'. Free-form prose explaining why the specialist didn't produce findings. Visible in the agent-behavior-log.

findings.description: All FAIL, PARTIAL, and PASS findings emitted by this specialist, in dispatch order. Empty array allowed (specialist ran cleanly but found nothing actionable; emit at least one PASS finding to confirm the specialist actually executed).

notes.description: Free-form specialist commentary that doesn't rise to a finding. Examples: 'No related-products carousel exists on this page (factually true; not a finding because absence is not always a defect on PDPs).' or 'Could not determine canonical URL — head section was malformed in this DOM slice.' Synthesizer may quote when integrating; reconciler ignores for validation. Relaxed to 1200 chars per item after Phase B/C/D testing showed specialists naturally write paragraph-length notes when documenting cross-cluster signals or degraded-condition reasoning.

telemetry.description: Optional cost/quality telemetry the specialist self-reports. Used by Phase 2 parity testing and the substantive canary 'finding-stability' assertion. additionalProperties: true (relaxed from false after Phase B/C/D testing showed specialists naturally self-report cluster-specific metrics like baton_elements_referenced, sections_routed_to_cluster, findings_by_verdict). Canonical fields below remain documented; specialists may add others.

reference_files_read.description: Reference filenames the specialist actually read (subset of the cluster reference pack), OR an integer count of files read. Array form is canonical and lets the synthesizer weight evidence; integer form is accepted for specialists that only have count-level information. Canary can verify the specialist read what it was supposed to.

allOf.description: When status='skipped', skip_reason is required and findings must be empty.
When status='complete' or 'partial', findings must contain at least one entry. A specialist that ran but emits zero findings should produce one PASS finding to confirm execution.
Ethics emissions (cluster='ethics') use device='page'. The ethics subagent runs once on the union of both devices; cluster auditors run per-device.
Cluster auditor emissions (cluster != 'ethics') use device='desktop' or device='mobile', never 'page'.

Every finding's cluster field MUST equal this emission's cluster field. Specialists cannot emit findings tagged for other clusters; cross-cluster integration happens in the synthesizer, not at emission time.

findings.title: ATC Button Color Blends Into Page

evidence_anchors.context: Add to Cart button uses #6B6B6B against white card; matches three other mid-grey UI elements above the fold.

findings.observation: The Add to Cart button uses a mid-grey fill that matches three other interactive elements within the same viewport. Without a distinct primary-action color, the button has no visual claim to being the most important action on the page.
findings.recommendation: If the brand has a primary accent color (the SlingMods red), apply it as the Add to Cart button background and reserve that color for purchase actions only.
findings.why_this_matters: On mobile product pages, the Add to Cart button is the only conversion action that matters; if it doesn't visually own the page, every conversion costs more attention than it should.

notes: Hero image renders cleanly above the fold; no LCP-impact CTA conflicts noted on this template.

examples.skip_reason: Cluster context for post-purchase contained zero routed sections after empty-slice pruning. Specialist not dispatched to avoid forcing thin findings on a $69.95 PDP.
